seed_instruction: Design a one-year plan for mastering a musical instrument, including practice schedules and milestone goals.
org_constraint: The plan must consist of exactly 12 paragraphs, each containing at least 3 sentences.
new_constraint: The response must consist of at most 30 sentences.
instruction: Design a one-year plan for mastering a musical instrument, including practice schedules and milestone goals. The plan must consist of exactly 12 paragraphs, each containing at least 3 sentences. The response must consist of at most 30 sentences.
response:
To begin your journey, select an instrument that resonates with your personal passions. Research the necessary equipment and find a qualified teacher for initial guidance. This foundational phase ensures you develop correct posture and basic techniques.

Month one focuses on learning the fundamental notes and basic scales. You should practice for thirty minutes daily to build muscle memory. Consistency is more important than duration during these early weeks.

By month two, you will start exploring simple melodies and basic rhythms. Focus on steady timing by using a metronome during every session. Record your progress to hear how your tone improves over time.

Month three introduces basic music theory and reading sheet music. Understanding the logic behind the notes will accelerate your learning speed. Try to identify different keys and common chord progressions.

During month four, you should begin practicing your first complete beginner pieces. Break these songs into small sections to master each part individually. Aim to play one full song without stopping by the end of the month.

Month five is dedicated to refining your technique and dynamic control. Work on playing softly and loudly to add emotional depth to your music. Practice scales at varying speeds to increase your dexterity.

In month six, you will reach the halfway point and tackle intermediate pieces. These songs require more complex fingerings and sustained concentration. Seek feedback from your teacher to correct any developing bad habits.

Month seven focuses on ear training and learning to play by ear. Try to mimic simple melodies you hear on the radio or in films. This develops a deeper connection between your brain and your hands.

Month eight is the time to explore different genres and styles. Experiment with jazz, classical, or pop to find your unique musical voice. Diversify your repertoire to keep your practice sessions engaging.

Month nine involves increasing your practice time to one hour per day. Focus on endurance and maintaining high quality during longer performances. Start preparing a small setlist of your favorite pieces.

Month ten is for polishing your performance skills and overcoming stage fright. Practice playing in front of friends or family to build confidence. Focus on your facial expressions and overall stage presence.

In the final month, perform a recital of your mastered pieces. Reflect on your growth from the first day to this final milestone. Continue practicing regularly to maintain your skills as a lifelong hobby.<turn|>
judged_response:
To begin your journey, select an instrument that resonates with your personal passions. Research the necessary equipment and find a qualified teacher for initial guidance. This foundational phase ensures you develop correct posture and basic techniques.

Month one focuses on learning the fundamental notes and basic scales. You should practice for thirty minutes daily to build muscle memory. Consistency is more important than duration during these early weeks.

By month two, you will start exploring simple melodies and basic rhythms. Focus on steady timing by using a metronome during every session. Record your progress to hear how your tone improves over time.

Month three introduces basic music theory and reading sheet music. Understanding the logic behind the notes will accelerate your learning speed. Try to identify different keys and common chord progressions.

During month four, you should begin practicing your first complete beginner pieces. Break these songs into small sections to master each part individually. Aim to play one full song without stopping by the end of the month.

Month five is dedicated to refining your technique and dynamic control. Work on playing softly and loudly to add emotional depth to your music. Practice scales at varying speeds to increase your dexterity.

In month six, you will reach the halfway point and tackle intermediate pieces. These songs require more complex fingerings and sustained concentration. Seek feedback from your teacher to correct any developing bad habits.

Month seven focuses on ear training and learning to play by ear. Try to mimic simple melodies you hear on the radio or in films. This develops a deeper connection between your brain and your hands.

Month eight is the time to explore different genres and styles. Experiment with jazz, classical, or pop to find your unique musical voice. Diversify your repertoire to keep your practice sessions engaging.

Month nine involves increasing your practice time to one hour per day. Focus on endurance and maintaining high quality during longer performances. Start preparing a small setlist of your favorite pieces.

Month ten is for polishing your performance skills and overcoming stage fright. Practice playing in front of friends or family to build confidence. Focus on your facial expressions and overall stage presence.

In the final month, perform a recital of your mastered pieces. Reflect on your growth from the first day to this final milestone. Continue practicing regularly to maintain your skills as a lifelong hobby.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The Model Response provides a detailed one-year plan for mastering a musical instrument, with 12 paragraphs as requested. However, the response does not adhere to the constraint of having at most 30 sentences, as the total number of sentences exceeds this limit. The model does not acknowledge the conflicting constraints (12 paragraphs with at least 3 sentences each vs. a maximum of 30 sentences) and directly provides a response to the instruction without addressing the issue.

**Answer**: 1
